get.summary:
GetTaskContext returns the current task context for an agent,
including claimed tasks, ready front, and board summary.

parameters.name: maxTokens
parameters.description: Maximum tokens for the context block (default: 500).

post.summary: CreateBoard creates a new kanban board.

get.summary: GetBoard retrieves a board with its lanes and task counts.
GetBlockedTasks returns tasks waiting on unfinished dependencies.
GetReadyFront returns tasks with all dependencies satisfied (ready to work on).

parameters.name: agentId
parameters.description: Optional: filter by agent capability (only tasks the agent can work on).

parameters.name: minPriority
parameters.description:
- TASK_PRIORITY_CRITICAL: P0: drop everything
 - TASK_PRIORITY_HIGH: P1: do next
 - TASK_PRIORITY_MEDIUM: P2: normal work
 - TASK_PRIORITY_LOW: P3: when time permits
 - TASK_PRIORITY_BACKLOG: P4: someday

get.summary: StreamTaskUpdates streams real-time task status changes.
ListTasks lists tasks with filtering and pagination.

parameters.name: status
parameters.description:
- TASK_STATUS_OPEN: Ready to be picked up
 - TASK_STATUS_IN_PROGRESS: Claimed and being worked on
 - TASK_STATUS_BLOCKED: Waiting on dependencies
 - TASK_STATUS_DONE: Completed
 - TASK_STATUS_DEFERRED: Parked intentionally
 - TASK_STATUS_CANCELLED: Won't do

parameters.name: priority
parameters.description:
- TASK_PRIORITY_CRITICAL: P0: drop everything
 - TASK_PRIORITY_HIGH: P1: do next
 - TASK_PRIORITY_MEDIUM: P2: normal work
 - TASK_PRIORITY_LOW: P3: when time permits
 - TASK_PRIORITY_BACKLOG: P4: someday

parameters.name: category
parameters.description:
- TASK_CATEGORY_RESEARCH: Information gathering, literature review
 - TASK_CATEGORY_ANALYSIS: Data analysis, investigation, diagnosis
 - TASK_CATEGORY_IMPLEMENTATION: Building, coding, creating artifacts
 - TASK_CATEGORY_REVIEW: Evaluating, auditing, quality checking
 - TASK_CATEGORY_WRITING: Documentation, reports, communication
 - TASK_CATEGORY_DECISION: Architectural decisions, trade-off evaluation
 - TASK_CATEGORY_INVESTIGATION: Debugging, root cause analysis
 - TASK_CATEGORY_PLANNING: Strategy, roadmap, decomposition

post.summary: CreateTask creates a new task on a board.

AddDependency creates a dependency edge between tasks.
Rejects if the edge would create a cycle.

patch.summary: UpdateTask updates mutable task fields (title, description, notes, etc.).

get.summary: GetTask retrieves a task by ID.

post.summary:
ClaimTask atomically claims a task for an agent session.
Fails if already claimed or not in OPEN status.

CloseTask marks a task as DONE with a reason.
If the task has an OutputPolicy, validates output before closing.
ReleaseTask releases a claim, returning the task to OPEN status.
TransitionTask changes task status with validation.
DecomposeTask uses an LLM to break a goal into a dependency DAG of subtasks.

output.description: Output to validate against the task's output_policy (if set).

description.description: Detailed description of what needs to be done.

objective.description: What "done" looks like — the desired end state.

approach.description: How to accomplish the objective (filled by agent or decomposer).

notes.description:
Running progress log. Agents append structured notes at milestones.
Survives context window compaction (rebuilt from DB each turn).

tags.description: Freeform tags for classification beyond the category enum.

assigneeAgentId.description: Agent currently working on this task.

claimedBySession.description: Session ID holding the claim lock.

closeReason.description: Reason for closing (completion summary, cancellation reason, etc.).

parentId.description: Parent task ID (for hierarchical decomposition: epic → task → subtask).

childIds.description: Child task IDs (populated by server on read).

entityIds.description: Graph memory entity IDs linked to this task.

compactedSummary.description: Summary created during compaction (replaces full description/notes).

outputPolicy.description: Output validation policy — validated before task can be closed as DONE.

task.description:
Task is a unit of cognitive work — domain-agnostic.
It can represent research, analysis, writing, decision-making, implementation,
review, or any decomposable objective.

updateMask.description: Fields to update. If empty, all mutable fields are updated.

v1BlockerList.description: BlockerList is a helper for the blocked tasks response.

success.description: False if task was already claimed or not in OPEN status.

validationFeedback.description: If output validation failed, this contains the feedback.

v1DecomposeStrategy.description:
DecomposeStrategy controls how the LLM decomposes a goal.

 - DECOMPOSE_STRATEGY_BACKWARD: Walk backward from the goal to prerequisites (beads model).
 - DECOMPOSE_STRATEGY_FORWARD: Plan sequentially from current state toward the goal.
 - DECOMPOSE_STRATEGY_PARALLEL: Maximize tasks that can run concurrently.

boardId.description: Target board for the created tasks.

parentTaskId.description: Optional: create subtasks under this parent task.

agentId.description: Agent ID performing the decomposition (for LLM selection).

blockers.description: For each blocked task, the IDs of tasks blocking it.

dependencies.description: Dependencies where this task is the from_task (things it depends on).

dependents.description: Dependencies where this task is the to_task (things depending on it).

outputSchema.description: JSON Schema string for structural validation (instant, free, deterministic).

acceptanceCriteria.description: Semantic validation criteria evaluated by LLM. Use {{output}} placeholder.

validatorAgentId.description: Optional: another agent evaluates the output.

judgeConfigId.description: Optional: judge config ID for multi-dimensional quality evaluation.

retryPolicy.description: Retry behavior when any validation fails.

v1OutputPolicy.description:
OutputPolicy defines how agent output is validated and optionally retried.
Attachable to workflow stages, tasks, parallel agent executions, or direct invocations.

maxRetries.description:
Maximum retries before falling back to default branch or graceful degradation.
0 = no retries (default behavior, preserves backwards compatibility).

includeValidValues.description:
Whether to include valid output values in the retry prompt.
For conditionals: lists valid branch keys.
For pipelines: includes the JSON schema or validation criteria.
For swarms: includes the vote format template.
Code treats unset (false) as true (include values by default).

sessionMode.description: Session behavior on retry. Default (UNSPECIFIED) = FRESH for backward compatibility.

feedbackTemplate.description:
Custom feedback template injected into the retry prompt.
Variables: {{error}}, {{previous_output}}, {{attempt}}, {{max_retries}}.
If empty, a default feedback template is used.

cooldownMs.description: Cooldown between retries in milliseconds. Default: 0 (no cooldown).

v1OutputRetryPolicy.description:
OutputRetryPolicy defines retry behavior when agent output doesn't match expectations.
Used by conditional executors (branch matching fails), pipeline executors
(validation_prompt or output_schema fails), and swarm executors (vote parsing fails).

v1RetrySessionMode.description:
RetrySessionMode controls how agent sessions are handled across retries.

 - RETRY_SESSION_MODE_UNSPECIFIED: Default: FRESH (preserves existing behavior — each retry gets a fresh session).
 - RETRY_SESSION_MODE_CONTINUE: Continue in the same session. Validation feedback is appended as a new
user message. The agent sees its previous attempt and what went wrong.
 - RETRY_SESSION_MODE_FRESH: Fresh session. New conversation with original prompt + error feedback.
 - RETRY_SESSION_MODE_ESCALATE: Escalate: first retry uses CONTINUE mode, subsequent retries use FRESH
with an upgraded LLM (switches to orchestrator_llm if available).

description.description: Detailed description of what needs to be done.

objective.description: What "done" looks like — the desired end state.

approach.description: How to accomplish the objective (filled by agent or decomposer).

notes.description:
Running progress log. Agents append structured notes at milestones.
Survives context window compaction (rebuilt from DB each turn).

tags.description: Freeform tags for classification beyond the category enum.

assigneeAgentId.description: Agent currently working on this task.

claimedBySession.description: Session ID holding the claim lock.

closeReason.description: Reason for closing (completion summary, cancellation reason, etc.).

parentId.description: Parent task ID (for hierarchical decomposition: epic → task → subtask).

childIds.description: Child task IDs (populated by server on read).

entityIds.description: Graph memory entity IDs linked to this task.

compactedSummary.description: Summary created during compaction (replaces full description/notes).

outputPolicy.description: Output validation policy — validated before task can be closed as DONE.

v1Task.description:
Task is a unit of cognitive work — domain-agnostic.
It can represent research, analysis, writing, decision-making, implementation,
review, or any decomposable objective.

workflowId.description: Optional: tie this board to a workflow execution.

v1TaskBoard.description: TaskBoard is a kanban board that groups tasks into lanes.

v1TaskBoardStats.description: TaskBoardStats provides aggregate counts for a board.

v1TaskCategory.description:
TaskCategory classifies the type of cognitive work. Domain-agnostic.

 - TASK_CATEGORY_RESEARCH: Information gathering, literature review
 - TASK_CATEGORY_ANALYSIS: Data analysis, investigation, diagnosis
 - TASK_CATEGORY_IMPLEMENTATION: Building, coding, creating artifacts
 - TASK_CATEGORY_REVIEW: Evaluating, auditing, quality checking
 - TASK_CATEGORY_WRITING: Documentation, reports, communication
 - TASK_CATEGORY_DECISION: Architectural decisions, trade-off evaluation
 - TASK_CATEGORY_INVESTIGATION: Debugging, root cause analysis
 - TASK_CATEGORY_PLANNING: Strategy, roadmap, decomposition

currentTasks.description: Task(s) the agent currently has claimed.

readyTasks.description: Tasks in the ready front (all deps satisfied, available to claim).

blockedByMe.description: Tasks blocked by the agent's current task(s).

blockingMe.description: Tasks blocking the agent's current task(s).

boardSummary.description: Compact text summary of board state (for LLM context injection).

v1TaskContext.description:
TaskContext is the agent's current awareness of its work state.
Injected into the system prompt each turn, rebuilt from the database.

v1TaskDependency.description: TaskDependency is a directed edge in the task dependency graph.

v1TaskDependencyType.description:
TaskDependencyType classifies dependency edges in the task graph.

 - TASK_DEPENDENCY_TYPE_BLOCKS: to_task blocks from_task (from_task cannot start until to_task is DONE).
 - TASK_DEPENDENCY_TYPE_PARENT_CHILD: Hierarchical: from_task is a child of to_task.
 - TASK_DEPENDENCY_TYPE_DISCOVERED_FROM: Informational: from_task was discovered while working on to_task.
 - TASK_DEPENDENCY_TYPE_RELATED: Informational: tasks are related but neither blocks the other.

status.description: Status that tasks in this lane have.

taskIds.description: Task IDs in display order within this lane.

v1TaskLane.description: TaskLane is a column in a kanban board, mapped to a task status.

v1TaskPriority.description:
TaskPriority indicates urgency. Lower number = higher urgency.

 - TASK_PRIORITY_CRITICAL: P0: drop everything
 - TASK_PRIORITY_HIGH: P1: do next
 - TASK_PRIORITY_MEDIUM: P2: normal work
 - TASK_PRIORITY_LOW: P3: when time permits
 - TASK_PRIORITY_BACKLOG: P4: someday

v1TaskStatus.description:
TaskStatus represents the lifecycle state of a task.

 - TASK_STATUS_OPEN: Ready to be picked up
 - TASK_STATUS_IN_PROGRESS: Claimed and being worked on
 - TASK_STATUS_BLOCKED: Waiting on dependencies
 - TASK_STATUS_DONE: Completed
 - TASK_STATUS_DEFERRED: Parked intentionally
 - TASK_STATUS_CANCELLED: Won't do

v1TaskUpdate.description: TaskUpdate is a streaming event for real-time task monitoring.
